steps.title: Calculate the threshold 5x+6 for each x value
steps.content:
To check if (x, y) satisfies y < 5x+6, I'll compute the boundary value for each x-coordinate in the tables. For x = 3, 5, 7:

$$5(3) + 6 = 15 + 6 = 21$$
$$5(5) + 6 = 25 + 6 = 31$$
$$5(7) + 6 = 35 + 6 = 41$$

A valid table must have all y-values strictly less than these thresholds.

steps.title: Test each table to find which satisfies the inequality
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B: (3, 17), (5, 35), (7, 37)

Check each pair:
  • (3, 17): Is \$17 < 21$? Yes ✓
  • (5, 35): Is \$35 < 31$? No ✗
Fails.

Testing choice C: (3, 25), (5, 35), (7, 45)
  • (3, 25): Is \$25 < 21$? No ✗
Fails.

Testing choice A: (3, 17), (5, 27), (7, 37)
  • (3, 17): Is \$17 < 21$? Yes ✓
  • (5, 27): Is \$27 < 31$? Yes ✓
  • (7, 37): Is \$37 < 41$? Yes ✓
All pairs satisfy the inequality. That matches choice A — A is correct.
